force.description: Should the queries be forced to load from the source. Default: `false`

annotation_layers.description: Annotation layers to apply to chart

descriptionColumns.description: Columns to use as the description. If none are provided, all will be shown.

hideLine.description: Should line be hidden. Only applies to line annotations

intervalEndColumn.description: Column containing end of interval. Only applies to interval layers

showLabel.description: Should the label always be shown

showMarkers.description: Should markers be shown. Only applies to line annotations.

sourceType.description: Type of source for annotation data

style.description: Line style. Only applies to time-series annotations

timeColumn.description: Column with event date or interval start date

value.description: For formula annotations, this contains the formula. For other types, this is the primary key of the source object.

applied_time_extras.description: A mapping of temporal extras that have been applied to the query

apply_fetch_values_predicate.description: Add fetch values predicate (where clause) to query if defined in datasource

columns.description: Columns which to select in the query.

column_order.description: Ordered list of column names for result ordering. Used to preserve user's column reordering (including mixed dimension columns and metrics)

having.description: HAVING clause to be added to aggregate queries using AND operator.

instant_time_comparison_range.description: This is only set using the new time comparison controls that is made available in some plugins behind the experimental feature flag.

relative_end.description: End time for relative time deltas. Default: `config["DEFAULT_RELATIVE_START_TIME"]`

relative_start.description: Start time for relative time deltas. Default: `config["DEFAULT_RELATIVE_START_TIME"]`

time_grain_sqla.description: To what level of granularity should the temporal column be aggregated. Supports [ISO 8601](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_8601#Durations) durations.

transpile_to_dialect.description: If true, WHERE/HAVING clauses will be transpiled to the target database dialect using SQLGlot.

where.description: WHERE clause to be added to queries using AND operator.

extras.description: Extra parameters to add to the query.

col.description: The column to filter by. Can be either a string (physical or saved expression) or an object (adhoc column)

grain.description: Optional time grain for temporal filters

isExtra.description: Indicates if the filter has been added by a filter component as opposed to being a part of the original query.

val.description: The value or values to compare against. Can be a string, integer, decimal, None or list, depending on the operator.

granularity.description: Name of temporal column used for time filtering.

granularity_sqla.description: Name of temporal column used for time filtering for SQL datasources. This field is deprecated, use `granularity` instead.

group_others_when_limit_reached.description: When true, groups remaining series into an 'Others' category when series limit is reached. Prevents incomplete data.

groupby.description: Columns by which to group the query. This field is deprecated, use `columns` instead.

having.description: HAVING clause to be added to aggregate queries using AND operator. This field is deprecated and should be passed to `extras`.

is_rowcount.description: Should the rowcount of the actual query be returned

metrics.description: Aggregate expressions. Metrics can be passed as both references to datasource metrics (strings), or ad-hoc metricswhich are defined only within the query object. See `ChartDataAdhocMetricSchema` for the structure of ad-hoc metrics.

orderby.description: Expects a list of lists where the first element is the column name which to sort by, and the second element is a boolean.

post_processing.description: Post processing operations to be applied to the result set. Operations are applied to the result set in sequential order.

options.description: Options specifying how to perform the operation. Please refer to the respective post processing operation option schemas. For example, `ChartDataPostProcessingOperationOptions` specifies the required options for the pivot operation.

row_offset.description: Number of rows to skip. Default: `0`

series_columns.description: Columns to use when limiting series count. All columns must be present in the `columns` property. Requires `series_limit` and `series_limit_metric` to be set.

series_limit.description: Maximum number of series. Requires `series` and `series_limit_metric` to be set.

series_limit_metric.description: Metric used to limit timeseries queries by. Requires `series` and `series_limit` to be set.

time_range.description:
A time rage, either expressed as a colon separated string `since : until` or human readable freeform. Valid formats for `since` and `until` are: 
- ISO 8601
- X days/years/hours/day/year/weeks
- X days/years/hours/day/year/weeks ago
- X days/years/hours/day/year/weeks from now

Additionally, the following freeform can be used:

- Last day
- Last week
- Last month
- Last quarter
- Last year
- No filter
- Last X seconds/minutes/hours/days/weeks/months/years
- Next X seconds/minutes/hours/days/weeks/months/years

time_shift.description: A human-readable date/time string. Please refer to [parsdatetime](https://github.com/bear/parsedatetime) documentation for details on valid values.

timeseries_limit.description: Maximum row count for timeseries queries. This field is deprecated, use `series_limit` instead.Default: `0`

timeseries_limit_metric.description: Metric used to limit timeseries queries by. This field is deprecated, use `series_limit_metric` instead.

additionalProperties.description: The value of the query parameter

url_params.description: Optional query parameters passed to a dashboard or Explore  view

where.description: WHERE clause to be added to queries using AND operator.This field is deprecated and should be passed to `extras`.
